data.output:
ID:       ddx-937de9fc
Title:    skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and ddx plugin commands honor both
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config, area:plugin, area:cli, kind:feature, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:skills-personas
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-602ae31a
Deps:     ddx-06cbaa90, ddx-04566a8f, ddx-06f820f4, ddx-64ecd687, ddx-c10d5af2, ddx-2bb4e0a5, ddx-b55fd606, ddx-a7ca3f7b, ddx-4c58ea3e
Desc:     PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this lands.
Accept:   1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.

status: open
reason: Parent bead is too large for a single execution pass and was already decomposed into six children. The implementable work lives in the children; this parent should not be implemented directly.
orchestrator_action: decompose

DECOMPOSITION (already recorded in parent notes; children exist with dep edges back to ddx-937de9fc):

- ddx-18e55cd2 — registry: global plugins dir resolution + project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence
- ddx-aedb009d — install/update: add --global writing to XDG global plugins + agent-tier skill links
- ddx-7c3893ad — install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path()
- ddx-c772fdf9 — init: add --global umbrella install; plain init stops installing plugins (lazy-resolve)
- ddx-ac6d4c22 — doctor: report Global Install and Project Install plugin layers
- ddx-406ae97b — docs: ADR skill-install-topology + CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md install precedence

WHY DECOMPOSE (Step 0 size check):
- 13 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems: registry resolution, install, update, init, doctor, and docs.
- AC mixes design (ADR), implementation (flags + path resolution), integration tests (six named Test* funcs across cmd and registry), and docs (CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, ADR).
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces (--global surface, lazy-resolve, two-layer doctor reporting).
- Estimated change far exceeds ~500 lines across ~5+ packages.

NOTE FOR OPERATOR (design conflict to resolve before children execute):
The bead's premise reintroduces a `--global` install surface and home-directory (~/.claude/skills, ~/.agents/skills) install paths. CLAUDE.md and ADR-027 (skill-install-topology) currently state these were *retired* in favor of project-local-only installs, and memory `feedback_no_local_replace` reinforces the project-local stance. The bead text claims it overrides CLAUDE.md, but this is a substantive architectural reversal of a recorded ADR decision. The docs child (ddx-406ae97b) explicitly proposes updating ADR/CLAUDE.md, which is the right place to ratify or reject the reversal. Recommend confirming the reversal is intended before the implementation children (ddx-18e55cd2, ddx-aedb009d, ddx-c772fdf9) land code that contradicts ADR-027.

data.text:
Done. Returning control to the orchestrator.

**Outcome: decomposition (no implementation).**

Bead **ddx-937de9fc** is a feature-sized parent that fails the Step 0 size check (13 ACs across registry / install / update / init / doctor / docs) and was **already decomposed** in a prior pass. All six children exist with dependency edges back to the parent and are recorded in the parent's notes:

- `ddx-18e55cd2` — registry resolution + precedence
- `ddx-aedb009d` — install/update `--global` + agent-tier links
- `ddx-7c3893ad` — project install in-tree vs convention
- `ddx-c772fdf9` — init `--global` / lazy-resolve
- `ddx-ac6d4c22` — doctor two-layer reporting
- `ddx-406ae97b` — ADR/CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md docs

I did **not** re-create children (they exist) and did **not** implement, per "decomposition alone is success; do not mix it with implementation." I wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` in the bundle path with `status: open` + `orchestrator_action: decompose` and the child IDs. No commit (no source changes; git clean).

**Flagged for the operator:** this bead reintroduces a `--global` surface and `~/.claude` / `~/.agents` home-dir install paths that CLAUDE.md and ADR-027 say were *retired*, and which memory `feedback_no_local_replace` reinforces. The bead asserts it overrides CLAUDE.md, but that's an architectural reversal of a recorded ADR. Worth confirming intent before the implementation children land code contradicting ADR-027 — the docs child (`ddx-406ae97b`) is the natural place to ratify or reject it.
